root_text.an6.77:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo uttari manussadhammaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanavisesaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.77:1.3: Muṭṭhassaccaṁ, asampajaññaṁ, indriyesu aguttadvārataṁ, bhojane amattaññutaṁ, kuhanaṁ, lapanaṁ.
root_text.an6.77:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo uttari manussadhammaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanavisesaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.77:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo uttari manussadhammaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanavisesaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.77:2.3: Muṭṭhassaccaṁ, asampajaññaṁ, indriyesu aguttadvārataṁ, bhojane amattaññutaṁ, kuhanaṁ, lapanaṁ.
root_text.an6.77:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo uttari manussadhammaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanavisesaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.77:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up six qualities you can’t realize a superhuman distinction in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones.
translation_text.an6.77:1.3: Lack of mindfulness and situational awareness, not guarding the sense doors, eating too much, fawning, and flattery.
translation_text.an6.77:1.4: Without giving up these six qualities you can’t realize a superhuman distinction in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones.
translation_text.an6.77:2.1: But after giving up six qualities you can realize a superhuman distinction in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones.
translation_text.an6.77:2.3: Lack of mindfulness and situational awareness, not guarding the sense doors, eating too much, fawning, and flattery.
translation_text.an6.77:2.4: After giving up these six qualities you can realize a superhuman distinction in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones.”
